evidence.epsilon_authority_scope_valid_time: pretask procedure transfer v4 internal calibration only
evidence.zeta_authority_scope_valid_time: pretask procedure transfer v4 internal calibration only

findings.summary: Both frozen source corpora expressly limit their authority and every proposition to v4, while v5 invokes them as the normative basis. Exact-byte reuse preserves the exclusion; it does not extend valid time to v5.

evidence.public_contract_phrase: using exactly these public field types

findings.summary: The checker ignores all unknown top-level and row fields. An otherwise canonical artifact carrying condition_id=reference_procedure plus an answer-hint payload passes, despite the exact public field contract and requested condition-leakage gate.
For t6v1, final_state {x: true} passes against expected {x: 1}. Raw Python equality is not strict JSON semantic equality, so the claimed typed consequence check is false.

evidence.source_statement: Rollback discards the target transaction mutations and all still-open descendants, recording their identifiers in rollback order.

findings.summary: The builder deriver permits rollback only when the target is the innermost transaction. Z-P3 expressly defines rollback of a target with still-open descendants. Builder derivation and builder preflight share this implementation, so their agreement cannot detect the defect.

required_disposition: Do not execute model/provider rows. Create a new prospectively frozen version rather than editing frozen v5 bytes: issue an explicit source-authority amendment or versioned v5 source rule; reject unknown fields at every object level; compare JSON recursively with strict type identity; independently implement and mutation-test Z-P3 ancestor rollback plus valid alternatives; then repeat an independent commit-bound freeze review.
